options_considered.name: C · RL-primary with LP fallback circuit

recommendation_rule: highest-strength option wins; ties broken by option order in options[]; strength order strong > conditional > moderate > weak > unsupported

rankings.rule_trace: C-012 ('no retrieved RL-primary production evidence') is the only claim the argument map links to the recommended option (05_argument_map.mmd path C012 --> X008 --> A; A maps to options[] 'A · Stay with LP/MIP/CP-SAT', strength strong). Flipping C-012 supported->unsupported drops A one tier from strong to moderate, tying option B (moderate); options[] order then keeps A the winner. The recommended option's strength tier changes but its identity does not, so this is contributing, not pivotal.

C-003 links to option B, which the argument map supports with five claims (C-003, C-005, C-008, C-010, C-014). Flipping C-003 leaves B with four supporting claims (still >=1), so B stays moderate; option A remains the winner with an unchanged strength tier.
C-014 is one of option B's five supporting claims. Flipping it leaves B with >=1 supporting claim, so B's tier is unchanged and the recommended option (A) and its tier are unaffected.
